# Signal Anatomy by Domain

> **Research boundary:** This material is for analysis, ethics, detection, and academic writing. It is not an operational guide for deceptive growth or platform manipulation.

This file explains exactly what kind of evidence is being distorted in each domain.

| Domain | Proxy signal | Why people trust it | How it is distorted at a research level | Value extracted |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Short-form video | Views, reposts, comments, repeated presence | Visibility feels like cultural relevance | Coordinated or paid distribution creates repeated exposure that appears spontaneous | Creator status, subscriptions, platform migration, brand deals |
| Music trends | Audio reuse, clips, comments, fan discourse | Trends feel crowd-driven | Distributed content and accounts create the perception of movement | Streams, playlisting, press, ticket interest |
| Reddit | Upvotes, comments, peer discussion | Reddit feels less commercial than ads | Native-format posts make promotion look like discovery | Installs, wishlists, credibility, discussion |
| Reviews | Star ratings, review count, testimonial tone | Reviews appear like independent experience | Fake, edited, filtered, incentivized, or selectively invited reviews alter perceived quality | Sales, ranking, trust |
| App stores | Ratings, installs, chart rank, search placement | Stores imply review and popularity | Fake reviews, incentivized installs, chart manipulation, deceptive submissions | Installs, subscriptions, data capture |
| Product Hunt | Launch points, comments, rank | Startup community rank feels like early validation | Coordinated upvotes or pressured engagement convert social graph into rank | Badges, press, investor proof |
| GitHub | Stars, followers, forks, activity | Developer interest implies quality | Automated or inauthentic starring/following inflates perceived adoption | Trust, hiring, investor interest, adoption |
| Google Maps | Local ratings and reviews | Local reviews imply real-world experience | Incentives, multi-account posts, fake experience, review suppression | Foot traffic, bookings, calls |
| Ads | Clicks, leads, conversions | Dashboards imply measurable demand | Invalid traffic or fake leads creates dashboard volume without buyer intent | Agency fees, channel budget, scam conversions |
| Mobile attribution | Click-to-install attribution | Attribution systems assign causality | Hijacking/flooding shifts credit to wrong source | Commission, media spend, budget control |
| Streaming | Streams, listener counts, catalog activity | Listening implies demand | Bots or manipulated streams mimic real listening | Royalties, charting, recommendations |
| Startups | ARR, waitlist, user count, launch rank | Metrics compress uncertainty | Numbers are inflated, undefined, cherry-picked, or used out of context | Funding, hiring, press, customer trust |
| Compliance | Certifications, audit claims, templates | Compliance implies institutional trust | Ambiguous claims overstate assurance or shift responsibility | Enterprise sales, investor confidence |
| Dark patterns | User actions, opt-ins, purchases | Actions look voluntary | Interface design hides friction, costs, defaults, or pressure | Revenue, data, retention |

## Research note

A metric is not automatically false because it is high. The question is whether it reliably represents the underlying reality it claims to represent.
